short_explanation: Flirtatious opener with no price, shortcode, or premium number. Personal message and adult-chat lure are both consistent with the text.
070 number with no tariff disclosure. UK 070 is both a personal follow-me range and a range abused by premium redirect scams; judged on the same standard as T3390.
Missed-call alert quoting a 070 number without tariff disclosure; same standard as T2710.
The ROMCAPspam prefix may be residue of a real horoscope campaign or a corpus annotation artifact; the body itself is ordinary warm chat.
Truncated text implying adult content, but no tariff, number, or shortcode survives in the row.
Requests address and date of birth for a product trial. Genuine recruitment by an acquaintance and cold data collection are both readable.
Club ticket notice; spam status depends on whether the recipient is a member who opted into club messages.
UNICEF disaster appeal. Charity solicitation is a genuine policy carve-out question, not a commercial broadcast.
Personal web page shared with a request for opinions. Depends on sender relationship; no charge or commercial mechanism. The 1/1 marker indicates a single-part message, not a chain.
Meta commentary demonstrating an anonymous SMS service to the recipient; sender intent cannot be recovered.
Service onboarding prompt asking for age and gender. Cannot distinguish a subscription the user initiated from a cold enrolment lure.
Picture link with no tariff. A legitimate photo-sharing notification and a marketing link are both consistent.
Teaser campaign with a URL but no price, number, or reply keyword; commercial intent is implied rather than evidenced.
Truncated fragment that reads as a personal thank-you; too little text survives to decide.
"I liked the new mobile" is ordinary ham. Word and character probabilities straddle 0.5 and neighbours are mixed, but predictive uncertainty alone is neither label error nor semantic ambiguity.
"Waiting for your call." is ordinary ham. The word model scores it 0.689 on shallow lexical overlap with call-to-action spam; no neighbour opposes the label.
Standalone observational joke about driving; no solicitation, contact channel, or charge.
One-line joke about tattoos; no solicitation. Held-out row, flagged only.
Divorce Barbie joke; self-contained punchline with no commercial mechanism.
Child-versus-teenager joke; conversational, no action requested.
Recipient asking what to do after a prize message; a victim query, not a solicitation. Held-out row, flagged only.
Personal reply from a named sender about a debt collector; no solicitation.
British joke: a dartboard with no doubles or trebles is useless. No real transaction intent or contact detail.
Stolen police toilet pun; no commercial content.
Personal question about having received dating spam; discussing spam is not sending it.
Personal rant about a disputed mobile bill; complaint, not solicitation.
Quotes a scam opener in order to warn the recipient about it; meta-warning, not the scam.
Reads as the user reporting money lost to shortcode 88066 and asking for help.
Near-duplicate of T2172; same help request about shortcode 88066.
Commentary on network billing responsibility; no solicitation. The 0A$NETWORKS prefix remains unexplained.
Complaint about receiving paid ringtone texts. Only one automated signal opposes; retained on policy reading and consistency with T1798 and T4296.
